24 - QA 10.mp4
I heard you mentioned like a three-strike rule. Is that what you did with like staff or clients that weren't aligned? Did you fire right away or did you kind of... Yeah, I think it's a situational basis, right? I mean, there was some where it was past three, and it was some that was instant. I think there's no real rule of thumb. I think if they don't fit the core values and they're not living that, then they can't be in your organization no matter at what cost, whether it's one strike or 15. Got it. How did you share the upside with your team? I visually, so I mapped out, if we hit these revenue targets, what that profitability would be. And then we set aside a lump sum of money, and then we start.
Divvying it up against you know what levels, what departments, and then we communicated it to everybody, listen guys. If we all hit this number, it's a group. This is what happens. Then we would meet with individual departments and said, 'If your department does this, then here's an extra.' What would happen, and then certain leaders that run departments, we would say, 'Okay, if you operated at this profitability and delivered things faster or whatever, here's another bonus for you So let's say, let's say you have a goal to make a million dollars. Like how much of that would you set aside as upside to share? I think it's all based on your profitability, right? So if you, let's say, you have 25% profitability and you're comfortable in operating at... you know, uh, 20, you know, maybe you allocate, you know, three to four of that, all right, guys. Thank you so much. Please put more questions down here, we'll get them answered, and we'll see in the next section.
